userMessage: Write a follow-up email to a client who hasn't responded to my proposal in 2 weeks.
agentResponse:
Subject: Following Up on Our Proposal

Hi [Client Name],

I hope you're doing well! I wanted to follow up on the proposal I sent over on [date]. I understand you're likely busy, but I'd love to hear your thoughts when you get a chance.

If you have any questions or would like to discuss any aspect of the proposal in more detail, I'm happy to jump on a quick call.

Looking forward to hearing from you!

Best regards,
[Your Name]
comment: Good tone, but could be more specific about what the proposal covers to jog their memory.
systemPrompt: You are a professional email writing assistant. Help users craft clear, effective, and persuasive emails for any situation.

userMessage: Now make it shorter and more direct. The client is a busy CEO.
agentResponse:
Subject: Quick Follow-Up: [Project Name] Proposal

Hi [Name],

Just circling back on the proposal I sent [date]. Happy to answer questions or jump on a 15-min call this week.

Best,
[Your Name]
comment: Perfect. This is exactly the kind of concise follow-up a CEO would actually read.

userMessage: Write a cold outreach email to a potential enterprise client in the SaaS space.
agentResponse:
Subject: Helping [Company] Scale Smarter

Hi [First Name],

I came across [Company] while researching leaders in the [industry] space — impressive growth over the past year.

We help SaaS companies like yours reduce churn by an average of 23% through predictive customer health scoring. [Similar Company] went from 8% to 3.2% monthly churn in 90 days using our platform.

Would a 20-minute call this week make sense to see if there's a fit?

Best,
[Your Name]
[Title] at [Your Company]
comment: Strong social proof and specific numbers. The subject line is good too.
systemPrompt: You are a professional email writing assistant. Help users craft clear, effective, and persuasive emails for any situation.

userMessage: Write an email declining a job offer professionally.
agentResponse:
Subject: Re: [Job Title] Offer — [Your Name]

Dear [Hiring Manager],

Thank you so much for offering me the [Job Title] position at [Company]. After careful consideration, I've decided to respectfully decline the offer. This was not an easy decision — I have great respect for the team and the work you're doing.

I hope our paths cross again in the future, and I wish you and the team continued success.

With appreciation,
[Your Name]
comment: Too vague — doesn't give any reason at all. Most people want a brief, honest reason. Also no mention of keeping the door open for future roles.
systemPrompt: You are a professional email writing assistant. Help users craft clear, effective, and persuasive emails for any situation.

userMessage: Write an angry email to a vendor who delivered the wrong order for the third time.
agentResponse:
Subject: Urgent: Third Incorrect Delivery — Immediate Resolution Required

Dear [Vendor Contact],

I'm writing to formally address a serious ongoing issue. This is now the third consecutive delivery where our order has been fulfilled incorrectly, causing significant disruption to our operations.

This level of repeated error is unacceptable and has eroded our confidence in your fulfillment process. We require:

1. Correct delivery within 48 hours at no additional cost
2. A written explanation of what caused these errors
3. A concrete plan to ensure this does not happen again

If we do not receive a satisfactory response by [date], we will need to explore alternative vendors and may seek compensation for the disruption caused.

I hope we can resolve this quickly and professionally.

[Your Name]
comment: Good structure with clear demands and a deadline. Firm but not unprofessional. Could add a line about the specific cost/impact to strengthen the case.
systemPrompt: You are a professional email writing assistant. Help users craft clear, effective, and persuasive emails for any situation.

userMessage: Translate the last email to French.
agentResponse:
Objet : Urgent : Troisième livraison incorrecte — Résolution immédiate requise

Cher/Chère [Contact fournisseur],

Je vous écris pour aborder formellement un problème récurrent et grave. Il s'agit désormais de la troisième livraison consécutive où notre commande a été exécutée incorrectement, causant des perturbations importantes à nos opérations.

Ce niveau d'erreurs répétées est inacceptable et a sérieusement entamé notre confiance dans votre processus de traitement des commandes. Nous exigeons :

1. Une livraison correcte sous 48 heures, sans frais supplémentaires
2. Une explication écrite des causes de ces erreurs
3. Un plan concret pour garantir que cela ne se reproduise pas

Si nous ne recevons pas de réponse satisfaisante d'ici le [date], nous devrons envisager des fournisseurs alternatifs et pourrons demander une compensation pour les perturbations occasionnées.

J'espère que nous pourrons résoudre cela rapidement et professionnellement.

[Votre nom]
comment: The agent translated correctly but this wasn't really an email writing task — it was a translation. The system prompt doesn't cover this. Should have clarified scope.
